Port.title: Port
Port.description: Represents a port in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Port entity.

location.description: The location of the port (e.g., city, country).

Passenger.title: Passenger
Passenger.description: Represents a passenger using the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Passenger entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the passenger.

lastName.description: The last name of the passenger.

email.description: The email address of the passenger.

phone.description: The phone number of the passenger.

Ship.title: Ship
Ship.description: Represents a ship in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ship entity.

capacity.description: The maximum passenger capacity of the ship.

status.description: The current status of the ship (e.g., 'In Service', 'Under Maintenance').

Staff.title: Staff
Staff.description: Represents a staff member in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Staff entity.

name.description: The name of the staff member.

role.description: The role of the staff member (e.g., Captain, Crew).

assignedShipId.description: The ID of the ship the staff member is assigned to.

assignedShipName.description: The denormalized name of the assigned ship.

Route.title: Route
Route.description: Represents a route in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Route entity.

distance.description: The distance of the route (e.g. in miles or kilometers).

passengerTypes.description: A list of passenger types available for this route.

Fare.title: Fare
Fare.description: Represents a fare for a specific route and passenger type.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Fare entity.

routeName.description: Denormalized name of the route for display purposes.

passengerType.description: The type of passenger for this fare.

Schedule.title: Schedule
Schedule.description: Represents a scheduled trip in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Schedule entity.

availableSeats.description: The number of seats available on this scheduled trip.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a passenger booking in the TransitFlow system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

bookingDate.description: The date the booking was made.

totalPrice.description: The total price of the booking.

MaintenanceRecord.title: MaintenanceRecord
MaintenanceRecord.description: Represents a maintenance record for a ship.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRecord entity.

startDate.description: The start date of the maintenance period.

endDate.description: The end date of the maintenance period.

definition.description: Stores port information. Access control: read access open to all users, write access restricted to authorized personnel.

params.name: portId
params.description: The unique identifier of the port.

definition.description: Stores passenger information. Path-based ownership: Only the passenger (or an admin) can access their own document.

params.name: passengerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the passenger.

definition.description: Stores ship information. Access control: restricted to authorized personnel (e.g., admins, maintenance staff).

params.name: shipId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ship.

definition.description: Stores staff information. Access is restricted to authorized personnel.

params.name: staffId
params.description: The unique identifier of the staff member.

definition.description: Stores route information. Access control: read access potentially open to all users, write access restricted to authorized personnel.

params.name: routeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the route.

definition.description: Stores fare information. Access control: read access open to all users, write access restricted to authorized personnel.

params.name: fareId
params.description: The unique identifier of the fare.

definition.description: Stores schedule information. Access control: read access potentially open to all users, write access restricted to authorized personnel.

params.name: scheduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the schedule.

definition.description: Stores booking information. Access control: restricted to the passenger who made the booking or authorized personnel. Includes denormalized `passengerId` for authorization independence.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the booking.

definition.description: Stores maintenance records for ships. Access control: restricted to authorized maintenance personnel.

params.name: maintenanceRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier of the maintenance record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the TransitFlow application's requirements for managing passengers, ships, routes, schedules, bookings, and maintenance records. The structure prioritizes authorization independence to ensure secure and scalable operations. Path-based ownership is used for user-specific data (passengers), while collaborative data (if any in future) will leverage membership maps. Structural segregation is employed to maintain a homogeneous security posture within collections.

Authorization Independence: Denormalization is applied where necessary to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. For instance, if access to Bookings needed to be restricted to passengers who made them, the `passengerId` is used in the Booking document, avoiding the need to `get` the Passenger document in rules.

QAPs Support:  Segregation ensures that list operations are secure. For example, listing ships does not require filtering based on complex rules; all documents in the `/ships` collection have the same basic access control requirements.
